User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the LETReviewBuddy application.

totalPoints.description: Total points accumulated by the user.

currentStreak.description: Current daily streak of the user.

lastStudyDate.description: The last date the user studied.

completedSessions.description: Number of pomodoro sessions completed by the user.

Question.title: Question
Question.description: Represents a question in the LET review.

difficulty.description: Difficulty level of the question (easy | medium | hard).

answer.description: The correct answer to the question.

choices.description: An array of choices for the question.

QuestionCategory.title: QuestionCategory
QuestionCategory.description: A document containing all questions for a specific category.

DailyProgress.title: DailyProgress
DailyProgress.description: Represents the daily progress of a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the daily progress entry, typically a combination of userId and date.

date.description: The date for which the progress is recorded.

easyCompleted.description: Indicates whether the easy challenge was completed.

mediumCompleted.description: Indicates whether the medium challenge was completed.

hardCompleted.description: Indicates whether the hard challenge was completed.

pointsEarned.description: Total points earned for the day.

pomodoroSessions.description: Number of pomodoro sessions completed on this day.

Pet.title: Pet
Pet.description: Represents a pet that can be unlocked.

pointsRequired.description: Number of points required to unlock the pet.

definition.description: Stores user profile data. Only the authenticated user can access their own profile data (private data access).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores all LET review questions, grouped by category. The categoryId will be 'gened' or 'profed'. Public read access is allowed.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The category of the questions ('gened' or 'profed').

definition.description: Stores the daily progress of each user. Only the authenticated user can access their own daily progress entries.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: date
params.description: The date for which the progress is recorded (format: YYYY-MM-DD).

definition.description: Stores the definitions of available pets. Allows public read access.

params.name: petId
params.description: The unique identifier of the pet.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to optimize for security, scalability, and the specific features of the LETReviewBuddy application. It adheres to the principle of Authorization Independence by avoiding hierarchical `get()` calls in security rules, which is achieved through denormalization where necessary. It also segregates data based on access requirements, ensuring a homogeneous security posture within each collection.  The use of path-based ownership and consistent naming conventions enhance clarity and debuggability.

*   **/users/{userId}**: Stores user profile data. This path ensures that only the authenticated user can access their own profile data (private data access).  
*   **/questions/{categoryId}**: Stores all LET review questions, grouped into two documents: 'gened' and 'profed'. This allows for efficient retrieval of all questions for a category in a single read, while still allowing public read access.
*   **/users/{userId}/dailyProgress/{date}**: Stores the daily progress of each user, using a composite key of userId and date. This structure facilitates easy querying of a user's daily progress and enforces ownership-based access control.
*   **/pets/{petId}**: Stores the definitions of available pets.

This structure supports the required Question Answering Patterns (QAPs) as follows:

*   **Secure List Operations**: Each collection (`users`, `questions`, `/users/{userId}/dailyProgress`, and `pets`) can be listed securely because the rules only depend on the authenticated user's ID (`request.auth.uid`) and/or the document data itself, and there are no dependencies on parent documents.
*   **Authorization Independence**: The structure ensures that security rules do not rely on fetching data from other documents (no `get()` calls), maintaining atomicity for operations like creating new daily progress entries or updating user profiles.
